title: Provide System Capability to Compare and Synchronize Internal Clocks
description: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source to generate time stamps for audit records.

checks.name: Windows Time Service is Running
checks.purpose: Verify the Windows Time (w32tm) service is running and set to start automatically

checks.name: NTP Source is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm the system is configured to sync time from an authoritative NTP source (domain hierarchy or external NTP server)

checks.name: Time Sync is Occurring Successfully
checks.purpose: Verify w32tm reports a successful last sync and the clock offset is within acceptable bounds

checks.name: Windows Time Service is Running
checks.purpose: Verify the Windows Time (w32tm) service is running and set to start automatically

checks.name: NTP Source is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm the server is configured to sync from the domain PDC emulator or a designated authoritative NTP source

checks.name: Time Sync is Occurring Successfully
checks.purpose: Verify w32tm reports a successful last sync and clock offset is within acceptable bounds (typically < 5 minutes for Kerberos)

checks.name: PDC Emulator Syncs to External Authoritative NTP Source
checks.purpose: On domain controllers, verify the PDC emulator role holder is configured to sync from an external authoritative NTP server (e.g., time.nist.gov or pool.ntp.org)

checks.name: NTP Daemon or Chrony is Running
checks.purpose: Verify chronyd, ntpd, or systemd-timesyncd is active and enabled at boot

checks.name: Authoritative NTP Server is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm /etc/chrony.conf or /etc/ntp.conf contains at least one authoritative NTP server (e.g., time.nist.gov or internal NTP hierarchy)

checks.name: Time Synchronization is Active and Within Bounds
checks.purpose: Verify chronyc tracking or ntpq -p reports successful sync with an offset within acceptable limits

checks.name: NTP Daemon or Chrony is Running
checks.purpose: Verify chronyd, ntpd, or systemd-timesyncd is active and enabled at boot

checks.name: Authoritative NTP Server is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm /etc/chrony.conf or /etc/ntp.conf contains at least one authoritative NTP server

checks.name: Time Synchronization is Active and Within Bounds
checks.purpose: Verify chronyc tracking or timedatectl reports successful NTP sync with an offset within acceptable limits

remediation: Enable and configure the Windows Time service to sync from the domain NTP hierarchy. Configure the PDC emulator to sync from an external authoritative NTP source. On Linux, install and configure chrony or ntpd with authoritative server entries. Verify sync status and clock offset.
